title: Building Inspection Services

narrative.executive_view: Building inspection is a modest AI-efficiency acquisition, not an AI replacement thesis. AI can reduce narrative, photo-labeling, scheduling and follow-up work, but the paid product remains a physical examination and a human written opinion. The 40-firm modeled EBITDA band is especially thin and founder/realtor relationships are often personal; buy only a multi-inspector firm with retained referral channels and credible normalized earnings [S1][S4].
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: ASHI defines a home inspection around visual examination of accessible installed systems and components, followed by a written report. That makes report drafting and administrative workflow the exposed layer, while site access, observation, testing and professional judgment remain human work. The supplied 5413 role mix is engineering-heavy rather than a faithful 541350 mix, so the residual is used to express the inspector-dominant real industry rather than pretending the parent mix measures it [S1][S2].
narrative.value_capture: Fees are generally a per-inspection service price rather than an hourly report-writing tariff, so a trusted local operator can retain some savings from faster report production. Capture is constrained by referral-driven local competition: standardized reports and readily available AI tools make the efficiency portable, and buyers can comparison-shop. The selected distribution and moat fractions deliberately exclude the physical human floor; that supports terminal demand, not capture [S1][S2].
narrative.adoption_timing: AI-assisted reporting is already commercially available, but broad effective deployment includes reliable field capture, templates, quality assurance and liability-safe review. I estimate current effective adoption below 50%, then a two-year path to half of operators using it on material workflows. This is an estimate rather than a survey of 541350 firms; the cited products establish capability, not penetration [S2].
narrative.consolidation_economics: The supplied band count is only 40, making a conventional broad roll-up difficult. Franchise systems and platforms can create repeatable workflows, but the central transferable asset is a staffed local brand and realtor/consumer funnel rather than a solo inspector's personal book. Generic service-business sold multiples provide a cautious valuation anchor, not an inspection-specific comp; they are reported as SDE/cash-flow multiples and are materially below a clean EBITDA-market benchmark [S4].
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is DURABLE but transaction-sensitive. ASHI requires the inspector to examine accessible systems and produce a written report, and licensing/certification requirements vary by state; AI cannot itself make the site visit. Yet demand is derivative of housing transactions and buyers sometimes waive inspection contingencies: NAR reported 25% waiving in May 2025, while existing-home sales were depressed. This supports durable paid demand with cyclicality, not immunity [S1][S3].
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest uncertainty is the severe 4-digit role-mix mismatch: construction inspectors are only 2.05% of supplied parent wages despite being the signature occupation here. Other material risks are transaction volumes, waiver behavior, personal referral concentration, state-by-state rules, and whether report-time is nearer one-fifth or one-third of inspector work. Generic service multiples are a broad proxy and should not set a purchase price without local comps and SDE-to-EBITDA normalization [S3][S4].

sources.title: ASHI Standard of Practice
sources.what_it_supports: Defines home inspection as visual examination of accessible systems/components and requires a written report.

sources.title: ReportWriter AI for InterNACHI inspectors
sources.what_it_supports: Shows an AI-oriented inspection reporting product exists; page was reopened but content was not fully retrievable, so it supports capability only.

sources.title: NAR May 2025 Realtors Confidence Index
sources.what_it_supports: Reports that 25% of responding buyers waived the inspection contingency in May 2025.

sources.title: BizBuySell Service Business Valuation Multiples
sources.what_it_supports: Reports 2021-2025 national sold service-business SDE earnings-multiple distribution and caveats on generic comparisons.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541350 ($1,864,147,476) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541350 ($3,995,784,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $146,226 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541350); class <5: 6,523 firms, avg revenue/firm $193,991 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $30,360 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 612 firms, avg revenue/firm $827,992 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $129,581 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 305 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,798,961 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $281,537 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 143 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,816,294 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $910,250 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 23 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,547,304 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,902,653 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 17 firms, avg revenue/firm $24,508,765 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,835,622 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 40
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI automates reporting/document workflow but not physical inspection; parent role mix is materially mismatched, so residual is a bounded industry reconstruction.
caveats: Supplied 4-digit 5413 mix is engineering-dominated and not representative of 541350.
AI reporting product availability is not adoption evidence.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Civil-engineering analysis and report drafting partially automate; verification persists.
Documentation automates, professional site judgment remains.
Scheduling, status and documentation are AI-exposed.
Drafting and report graphics are highly digital.
Admin automates but referral management and accountability remain.
Technical write-up automates; physical/engineering review remains.
Report narrative and photo annotation automate; the inspection itself does not [S1].
Coding is AI-augmented but integration/review remain.
Reconstructed inspector/admin-heavy residual combines partially automatable reports and workflow with physical field work [S1].

pi_dist.rationale: Per-inspection pricing can retain workflow savings initially, but local competition distributes a substantial share.
caveats: No inspection-specific pricing pass-through study was found.

pi_moat.rationale: Local reputation and referral channels protect some margin, but are personal and demand is transaction-sensitive.
caveats: Physical inspection requirement is excluded because it is terminal-demand support, not capture.

t50_years.rationale: Tools exist but evidence does not establish majority effective use; two years is a bounded workflow-adoption judgment.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Product availability supports nonzero usage but not penetration; selected value remains below 50%.
caveats: Vendor capability is not market adoption data.

historical_analogs.value: Mobile inspection software and digital report templates reduced documentation friction while preserving onsite inspection labor.
historical_analogs.rationale: ASHI's scope distinguishes inspection from written report.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: Owner-operator service-business demographics are only a broad proxy; no dedicated inspection-owner age survey was found.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No source in this packet documents an inspection-industry succession shortage.
caveats: Do not infer shortage from solo-firm prevalence.

active_consolidators.rationale: Franchise/platform activity indicates some organized buyers, but generic transaction data does not count inspection-specific acquirers.
caveats: Not a documented count of active 541350 consolidators.

buy_mult.rationale: Selected cautiously around broad service sold SDE multiples, adjusted conceptually for larger transferable firms.
caveats: S4 is national generic service SDE, not inspection EBITDA; conversion is judgmental.

exit_mult.rationale: A modest premium assumes staff, referral retention and standardized workflow; no AI multiple expansion is assumed.
caveats: Generic SDE proxy; exit depends on transferable relationships.

pricing_structure.value: Flat per-inspection fees, with ancillary tests and commercial/specialty work as add-ons.
pricing_structure.rationale: Written inspection reports are discrete deliverables; industry practice supports per-job rather than time-only economics.
caveats: No national fee schedule was used.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Moderate task savings and a two-year adoption path are consistent: field work remains nonautomatable even after reporting tools diffuse.

terminal_value.justification: Onsite visual examination and accountable written opinions remain paid work, although transaction volumes and inspection waivers pressure demand [S1][S3].

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No inspection-specific adoption, owner-age, or EBITDA multiple proxy is claimed as direct evidence; those selections are explicitly estimates.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Per-inspection pricing permits partial margin retention, but portable reporting tools and local fee competition imply material pass-through.

dataset_mismatch.description: The supplied 4-digit 5413 employment mix is engineering-dominated and materially understates inspectors in 541350.
sensitivity: Residual reconstruction drives the AI-replaceable-share result.
A true 6-digit mix could move V materially.

market_boundary.selection_basis: The score emphasizes the residential onsite inspection and report product; municipal enforcement and engineering firms are excluded.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Changes V directly through reconstructed residual.

reviewer_note: Use local inspection transaction comps, cohort-level adoption evidence, and referral-retention diligence before underwriting.

A.arithmetic: Python v3.1.2: A = 10 when t50=0 else min(10,10/t50); t50=2, A=5
